evals.prompt: What would a React expert say about our decision to use Redux for a simple todo app with 3 screens?
evals.expected_output: Single mode — one expert from React/Frontend domain. Focused perspective that pushes back on Redux for a simple app. One bold framing sentence, then AskUserQuestion with detail panels. No expert names in output.
expectations: Infers Single mode from 'what would [expert] say' signal
Selects expert from React / Frontend / TS / JS domain row in domain map
Reads the selected expert's profile from profiles/ before reasoning
One bold sentence framing the core insight appears before the AskUserQuestion — not zero text, not a paragraph
Presents via AskUserQuestion — not plain text output
Detail panels use only WHY IT MATTERS and TRADEOFF sections with ALL CAPS headers — no SUGGESTION, BLIND SPOT, POSITIONS, TENSION, or CONCERN headers
Detail panels are max 10 lines each with ~40 char lines
No expert names appear anywhere in user-facing output (labels, descriptions, detail panels, bold sentence)
Options include a 'Go deeper' option
Pushes back on Redux for a simple 3-screen app if the expert's documented positions would challenge it

evals.prompt: Panel: We're choosing between a monolith and microservices for a new B2B SaaS product. Team of 4 devs, launching in 3 months, expecting 100 initial users growing to 10K over a year.
evals.expected_output: Panel mode — 2-4 experts from different domains. Productive disagreement over monolith vs microservices. One bold framing sentence, then multiple concerns as AskUserQuestion options organized by decision dimension.
expectations: Infers Panel mode from 'Panel' keyword
Selects 2-4 experts from different domain rows (max 2 from same row)
Reads each selected expert's profile from profiles/ before reasoning
Experts disagree on the core question — if all experts agree easily, the wrong experts were selected
One bold sentence framing the core tension appears before the AskUserQuestion
Presents multiple concerns as AskUserQuestion options (not one monolithic answer)
Detail panels use only WHY IT MATTERS and TRADEOFF sections — no forbidden headers (POSITIONS, TENSION, CONCERN)
No expert names appear anywhere in user-facing output
Options include a 'Go deeper' option
Concerns organized by decision dimension, not by expert — no 'Expert A thinks X' framing

evals.prompt: Review our API design against best practices: REST endpoints for a task management system. We have GET/POST/PUT/DELETE for /tasks, nested /tasks/:id/comments, JWT auth, no rate limiting, no pagination, returns 200 for everything.
evals.expected_output: Review mode — 3-4 experts for breadth sweep. One bold framing sentence, then coverage concerns as AskUserQuestion options. Identifies specific gaps (rate limiting, pagination, status codes). Breadth over depth.
expectations: Infers Review mode from 'Review' keyword and 'against best practices' signal
Selects 3-4 experts crossing relevant domains (REST/APIs, Architecture, Security at minimum)
Reads each selected expert's profile from profiles/ before reasoning
One bold sentence framing the overall assessment appears before the AskUserQuestion
Identifies missing rate limiting, missing pagination, and incorrect status codes as specific concerns
Detail panels use only WHY IT MATTERS and TRADEOFF sections — no forbidden headers
No expert names appear anywhere in user-facing output
Options include a 'Go deeper' option
Breadth over depth — surfaces multiple concerns rather than deep-diving one

evals.prompt: I'm stuck on a race condition in our checkout flow. Orders sometimes get created twice when users double-click the submit button. I've tried disabling the button on click but it still happens. The problem is intermittent — maybe 1 in 50 submissions.
evals.expected_output: Unblock mode — 2-3 diagnostic experts. One bold sentence reframing the problem, then ranked next steps as AskUserQuestion options. Root cause diagnosis: server-side idempotency, not client-side button disable.
expectations: Infers Unblock mode from 'stuck on' signal and repeated failure description
Selects 2-3 experts from relevant diagnostic domains
Reads each selected expert's profile from profiles/ before reasoning
One bold sentence reframing the problem appears before the AskUserQuestion — shifts from client-side to server-side framing
Presents ranked next steps as AskUserQuestion options (not a text dump)
Detail panels use only WHY IT MATTERS and TRADEOFF sections — no forbidden headers
No expert names appear anywhere in user-facing output
Options include a 'Go deeper' option
Next steps are actionable and specific (not generic advice like 'add error handling')
